response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack and backend software engineer with 17+ years of experience across web, mobile, and embedded systems, but his background has negligible overlap with the Principal Performance Architect role. The position demands deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD systems, hardware-software co-design, and deep neural network training/inference optimization — none of which appear anywhere in his resume. His AI-related work at Google is limited to agentic tooling and MCP infrastructure, not silicon-level or DNN workload performance analysis. His performance engineering experience is confined to high-throughput web services. The domain mismatch is severe enough to make this candidacy non-viable regardless of his general engineering tenure.
strengths: 17+ years of broad engineering experience demonstrating strong adaptability and consistent progression to staff-level roles
Demonstrated performance-oriented systems engineering at scale — millions of HTTP requests per second using async I/O and low-latency techniques at Sociomantic
Current Google role includes AI-agent infrastructure and developer tooling, indicating some exposure to the broader AI ecosystem
concerns: No SoC, IP modeling, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or hardware-software co-design experience anywhere in the resume
No deep learning, neural network training/inference optimization, or AI accelerator performance analysis background
Skill set is entirely web and application-layer (TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB) with no ML systems tooling, chip simulation, or hardware instrumentation

key_factors.factor: No SoC or hardware architecture experience
No DNN or AI accelerator performance expertise
Strong general software engineering tenure at top-tier companies
